Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 3,028,236.32 was moved across the accounts in this case.
  • The largest source of funds was Pushpendra Pathak, which sent out Rs 1,364,355.33.
  • Money was routed through 2 intermediary accounts.
  • Pushpendra Pathak spread money to 272 different accounts.
  • Pushpendra Pathak collected money from 76 different accounts.
  • Funds finally accumulated in the account of MR. SACHIN SUMIT GUPTA.
  • Rs 10,242,102.00 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals.
  • The account to investigate first is PRADEEP YASH GHOSH.

4 · Findings by Account

PRADEEP YASH GHOSH strong
Account Number 8855611820 · Kotak Mahindra Bank · Score 1,659
Rank #1

This account is suspicious due to unusual money behaviour, including large credits and subsequent debits, structuring, and potential money laundering activities. The account's transactions exhibit patterns of round-value debits and pass-through routing. These activities warrant further investigation.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 899059.00 was traced into 6 subsequent debits, with the entire amount allocated and none remaining untraced. (+13 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — 45 credit transactions totalling 214998.00 were made on 2021-11-03, each below the individual cutoff, indicating potential structuring. (+3 more of this type)
  • Credit To Cash Out Chains weak — A credit of 32000.00 was followed by 3 ATM/cash withdrawals totalling 50302.35 within 2 days, exceeding the credit amount. (+6 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions ref:4632028839360165 share an account, direction, near-equal amount, and nearby date, suggesting potential duplicate transactions.
  • Pass Through Routing Account weak — The account processed 16664888.91 in combined inflow/outflow with a 100.0% throughput ratio, indicating potential pass-through activity.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 506 nodes and 1081 directed transaction edges was constructed, showing complex transaction patterns.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — 5 suspicious round-value debits totalling 2845000.00 were made, with the debits being multiples of 1000.

Fraud-Pattern Summary

The graph shows various patterns of potentially fraudulent transactions, with the most notable figure being 324 instances of Duplicate Verification. An investigator should take note of the high frequency of Duplicate Verification and the presence of other patterns such as Failed Reversed Transaction Detection and Structuring Smurfing Detection, which occurred 5 and 4 times respectively. The data also reveals 27 instances of Money Trail Tracing and 12 instances of Credit To Cash Out Chains, indicating busy patterns in these areas.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
